column3: Verify that the backend accepts the exact Swagger-documented example body when creating a plant
column4:
- Admin token is available
- Category with id=5 exists
column5:
1. Prepare a POST request to /api/plants/category/5 with the FULL Swagger example body exactly as shown in the Swagger UI example:
{
  ""id"": 0,
  ""name"": ""<unique-name>"",
  ""price"": 150,
  ""quantity"": 25,
  ""category"": {
    ""id"": 0,
    ""name"": ""Anthurium"",
    ""parent"": ""string"",
    ""subCategories"": [""string""]
  }
}

2. Set Authorization header with admin token
3. Send the POST request
4. Capture and compare response with Swagger UI
column6:
1. API response status code must be 201 (Created)
2. The backend must honour its own published API contract
3. Optional fields (id, category) must be silently ignored by the backend
4. The Swagger spec is the single source of truth; the example shown IS the contract
column7:
1. API returned HTTP status code 500 (Internal Server Error)
2. Backend crashed on its own Swagger-documented optional fields
3. Defect: backend violates its own published API contract by rejecting a body it itself documents as valid

column3: Verify that when optional Swagger fields are included  when creating a plant the backend returns 4xx not 5xx
column4:
- Admin token is available
- Category with id=5 exists
column5:
1. Prepare a POST request to /api/plants/category/5 with the FULL Swagger example body including optional fields (id and nested category)
2. Set Authorization header with admin token
3. Send the POST request
4. Capture and check response status code
column6:
1. API response status code must NOT be 500 (Internal Server Error)
2. If the backend cannot handle the optional fields, it must return 400 (Bad Request) with a clear validation message
3. A 500 is a server-side crash; client input — even if unexpected — must never cause the server to crash
4. This is an independent requirement from TC API_POST_PLT_ADM_006: even if the backend intentionally rejects the optional fields, the error code must be 4xx
column7:
1. API returned HTTP status code 500 (Internal Server Error)
2. The server crashed instead of returning a clean client error (4xx)
3. Defect: backend must never return 5xx for client-supplied input regardless of validity
